assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements.
The intragroup dividend receivable is a recognised intragroup monetary item denominated in a foreign currency.
The issue is limited to IFRS hedge accounting models relevant to foreign currency risk in consolidation.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to a particular risk affecting profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a separate top-level hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify net investment hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
